testCases.title: Status change triggers automatic alert to user
testCases.preconditions: Tracking code is registered. Dev confirms or simulates a status change in staging.
steps: Add a tracking code to the bot
Wait for the worker to poll (or ask dev to trigger a status change in staging)
testCases.expectedResult: Bot sends an unsolicited message with: previous status → new status, full updated event history. User does not need to send any message to receive this alert.
testCases.notes: Coordinate with dev to trigger a status change in staging. If not possible, test on a real parcel and wait.

testCases.title: Alert includes courier info when new status is Out for Delivery
testCases.preconditions: A tracked parcel transitions to status 40 (Out for Delivery) during a worker poll.
steps: Track a code that transitions to Out for Delivery
testCases.expectedResult: Alert includes courier name and courier phone number in addition to status change and event history.
testCases.notes: Per PRD 6.2 — courier info must be in the alert when status is Out for Delivery.

testCases.title: No status change → no alert sent
testCases.preconditions: A tracking code is registered. Its status has NOT changed since last poll.
steps: Wait for at least one full worker poll cycle
testCases.expectedResult: No message is sent by the bot. Chat remains silent. No duplicate or unnecessary alerts.
testCases.notes: Spamming users with repeated alerts when nothing changes is a critical UX bug.

testCases.title: Delivered parcel triggers alert with delivered status
steps: Track a code for a parcel near delivery
Wait for the worker to detect the delivered status
testCases.expectedResult: Bot sends an alert clearly indicating the parcel has been delivered.
testCases.notes: Confirm with dev whether delivered codes are auto-removed from tracking.

testCases.title: Multiple users tracking same code each receive their own alert
testCases.preconditions: Two different Telegram accounts both track the same code. Status changes during a poll.
steps: Account A adds a tracking code
Account B adds the same tracking code
Wait for a status change and worker poll
testCases.expectedResult: Both Account A and Account B independently receive the alert. Neither one is skipped.
testCases.notes: Per PRD architecture — each chat ID is an independent user context.
